issues.description: Slide 7 enumerates five specific hazard controls (eliminate, substitute, engineering, administration, PPE), but the ContentModel collapses this into a single hazard block whose text ('must be addressed using the hierarchy of controls to protect workers') is fabricated and not present on the slide. The five controls — safety-critical content and the basis of objective obj_03_1 — are entirely omitted from any block.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a list or key_point block capturing the five controls verbatim from slide 7, and remove or correct the fabricated 'hierarchy of controls' sentence to match source wording.

issues.description: q_08 asks workers to list all five hazard controls, but its only cited source block blk_03_02 does not list any controls; it only references a 'hierarchy of controls.' The correct answer (opt_a) cannot be verified from the cited block, and the rationale falsely claims blk_03_02 lists the five controls. This stems from the upstream fidelity omission.
issues.suggested_fix: After restoring the five-controls block in structure, re-cite that block as the source for q_08.

issues.description: q_09 (substitution scenario) cites only blk_03_02, which does not define substitution or any control type. The correct answer opt_c cannot be verified from the cited block; rationale misattributes a definition to blk_03_02.
issues.suggested_fix: Cite the (to-be-restored) block listing the five controls so substitution is defined in the source set.

issues.description: q_04 asks what must be done when an accident/incident/near miss occurs; the correct answer opt_b asserts they must be reported immediately. The cited blocks (blk_02_03, blk_02_04) cover accountability and following guidelines only — the reporting requirement lives in blk_02_05, which is not cited. The answer is unverifiable from the cited sources.

q_05 (near misses must be reported) cites only blk_02_04 ('Everyone is accountable for their health and safety performance'), which says nothing about reporting near misses. The correct answer 'False' cannot be verified from the cited block; the rationale references reporting content absent from the cited source.
issues.suggested_fix: Cite blk_02_05 (incident reporting callout) as the source for q_05.

issues.description: q_06 asks where the HSE Policy is found; the correct answer (beginning of the Program Manual) comes from blk_02_10, but q_06 cites blk_02_08 and blk_02_09, neither of which states the policy location. The rationale incorrectly attributes the location to blk_02_09. Answer unverifiable from cited blocks.
issues.suggested_fix: Replace cited blocks with blk_02_10 (or add it).

issues.description: q_07 cites only blk_02_11, which is merely the heading 'Accessing the Health and Safety Program Manual.' The 'ask questions if unsure' content is in blk_02_12, not cited. The correct answer opt_b cannot be verified from the cited heading; rationale references content not in the cited source.
issues.suggested_fix: Cite blk_02_12 as the source for q_07.
